name: Seediq
notes: STUB: the grapheme inventory of Seediq is NOT yet specified (graphemes/allophones are empty). Seediq (including the Truku/Taroko variety, which the ISO code trv names) is a Formosan (Austronesian) language of Taiwan, written in the Latin script under the Council of Indigenous Peoples' 2005 romanisation. Its three varieties (Tgdaya, Toda, Truku) differ phonologically, so a letter→IPA table would need to be variety-scoped. INPUT CONTRACT: Latin-script (official romanisation) Seediq/Truku text. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode taro1264), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode taro1264.

ancestors.notes: Austronesian — the classification node this language sits under.
